macro_events.event_summary: Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) cut its copper production guidance, sending its shares lower while competing copper producers rally on the implied tighter supply and higher copper prices.
primary_drivers: Reduced copper supply from a major producer tightens the global copper market, supporting spot prices
Peer copper miners with unchanged output capture higher realized prices without volume loss
Relative-value rotation out of FCX into pure-play copper peers

beneficiaries.rationale: Southern Copper is one of the largest pure-play copper producers with no guidance cut; a higher copper price directly flows to revenue/EBITDA given fixed cost base and stable volumes. Classic peer-rotation beneficiary when FCX disappoints.

Teck Resources has pivoted to a copper-focused producer (QB2 ramp); tighter copper market lifts realized prices and narrows the valuation gap as investors rotate from FCX into alternative large-cap copper exposure.

Ero Copper is a small-cap pure-play copper miner with high beta to copper prices; supply cut from FCX is directly bullish for spot copper and ERO's leverage to price is among the highest in the group.
Hudbay Minerals derives the majority of revenue from copper (Constancia, Copper Mountain); benefits from higher copper prices with no offsetting guidance issue.

losers.rationale: Direct subject of the event — cut copper production guidance reduces near-term volumes and EBITDA; lower revenue visibility despite higher spot price as the cut likely stems from operational/grade issues at their mines.
Commercial Metals and other copper/wire fabricators face higher input costs if copper prices rise on the supply cut; margin pressure until passed through to customers.

headlines: FCX Stock Dives On Copper Output Cut. These Stocks Are Rallying.
